metadata.title: 8th Pass Backlog Fix: All P0+P1 Issues from Debug Factorial Experiment
metadata.priority: P0+P1 — 37 issues accumulated across 8 debug factorial passes
metadata.approach: TDD: RED → GREEN → VERIFY → FIX → CHECKPOINT → CONVERGE

user-decisions.decision: #878 fix: GCS artifact store via Pulumi update
Metric keys: Central MetricKeys constants module
Pulumi: Modify Pulumi code directly (not gcloud CLI)
Env fallbacks: Fail loudly, no silent defaults
Stale YAMLs: Fix both production + HPO
E2E chain: Local mock test + cloud E2E plan
Tracker: GitHub issues + XML checklist with verification script
MLflow auth: Remove USERNAME/PASSWORD from train_factorial.yaml
Issues: Create GitHub issues for all 38 untracked findings

context.summary:
Across 8 debug factorial experiment passes, 37 P0+P1 issues were identified. These range from infrastructure blockers (GCS artifact store not configured, stale SkyPilot YAMLs pointing to GHCR/UpCloud) to silent code failures (hardcoded alpha/seed, swallowed exceptions, unwired functions) to validation gaps (no E2E chain test, no MetricKeys guard tests).
This plan addresses ALL 37 issues in 3 phases: Phase 1: Infrastructure (6 tasks) — unblocks the pipeline Phase 2: Code Fixes (24 tasks) — silent failures, metrics, config Phase 3: Validation (9 tasks) — tests, E2E, cloud verification
Every task follows TDD: write failing test FIRST, then implement the fix. Every task maps to one or more GitHub issues for tracking.

phase.name: Infrastructure
phase.description: Fix infrastructure blockers that prevent the debug factorial from running on GCP. These must be done first because Phase 2 code fixes cannot be validated end-to-end without working cloud infrastructure.

task.title: Pulumi MLflow GCS artifact store configuration
task.description:
MLflow on Cloud Run currently uses the default ./mlruns artifact store, not the GCS bucket (gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts). This means all artifacts written during GCP training jobs are stored on ephemeral Cloud Run disk and lost on redeploy. The Pulumi stack must configure MLflow's --default-artifact-root to point to the GCS bucket.
Root cause: The Cloud Run service definition in __main__.py does not pass --default-artifact-root gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts to the MLflow server command.
User decision #2: Fix via Pulumi code modification (not gcloud CLI). User decision #8: Modify Pulumi code directly.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that parses __main__.py AST and asserts the Cloud Run MLflow container args include --default-artifact-root with the GCS artifacts bucket URI. Test fails because the arg is missing. GREEN: Add --default-artifact-root gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts to the Cloud Run service template in __main__.py. Also ensure the MLflow service account has storage.objectAdmin on the artifacts bucket.
task.verification: 1. make test-staging passes with new test 2. pulumi preview shows the Cloud Run service update 3. After pulumi up: mlflow.get_artifact_uri() returns gs:// path

task.title: GCP GPU quota increase request to 4 GPUs
task.description:
Current GPUS_ALL_REGIONS quota is 1. The factorial experiment has 34 conditions; running them sequentially takes ~34x longer than necessary. A quota of 4 allows 4 parallel sky jobs launch invocations, reducing wall-clock time by ~4x.
User decision #3: Request increase to 4.
This is a manual GCP Console action (IAM > Quotas) but must be documented and tracked. The Pulumi config should also record the expected quota for preflight checks.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that reads gcp_quotas.yaml and asserts gpus_all_regions >= 4. Test fails because current value is 1. GREEN: Update gcp_quotas.yaml to expected_gpus_all_regions: 4. Update preflight_gcp.py to check actual quota via gcloud CLI. Document the quota request process in gcp-setup-tutorial.md.
task.verification: 1. gcloud compute project-info describe --project=minivess-mlops shows quota >= 4 2. scripts/preflight_gcp.py passes quota check

task.title: Fix train_production.yaml — GAR image + GCS data path
task.description:
train_production.yaml still references: - GHCR image (ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest) instead of GAR (europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest) - UpCloud/RunPod cloud config instead of GCP - Old data strategy (S3 public bucket) instead of GCS DVC
This YAML is stale and will fail on any GCP production launch. User decision #11: Fix both production + HPO YAMLs.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Existing yaml_contract_enforcement test should already catch GAR mismatch. If not, add assertion that train_production.yaml uses GAR image. GREEN: Rewrite train_production.yaml to mirror train_factorial.yaml's infrastructure pattern: GAR image, GCP cloud, L4 GPU, GCS DVC pull, GCS checkpoint mount. Keep the production-specific config (100 epochs, 3 folds, full dataset).
task.verification: 1. scripts/validate_yaml_contract.py passes for train_production.yaml 2. sky check --yaml deployment/skypilot/train_production.yaml validates 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Fix train_hpo.yaml — GAR image + GCS data, remove UpCloud
task.description:
train_hpo.yaml references: - GHCR image instead of GAR - RunPod cloud + RTX 4090 instead of GCP + L4 - UpCloud S3 DVC credentials (DVC_REMOTE_URL, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID)
UpCloud was sunset on 2026-03-16. All UpCloud references must be removed. User decision #11: Fix both production + HPO YAMLs.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Add yaml contract test asserting train_hpo.yaml uses GAR image and GCP cloud, and does NOT contain UpCloud/AWS_ACCESS_KEY references. GREEN: Rewrite train_hpo.yaml to use GAR image, GCP cloud, L4 GPU, GCS DVC pull. Remove all UpCloud S3 env vars. Keep HPO-specific config (Optuna trial args, HPO_CONFIG env var).
task.verification: 1. grep -r "UpCloud\|upcloud\|AWS_ACCESS_KEY" deployment/skypilot/train_hpo.yaml returns 0 hits 2. scripts/validate_yaml_contract.py passes 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Remove MLflow auth env vars from train_factorial.yaml
task.description:
train_factorial.yaml lines 77-78 contain: MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME} MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD}
MLflow 3.x on Cloud Run uses IAM authentication (service account), NOT HTTP basic auth. These env vars are vestigial from MLflow 2.x and cause confusion. The setup diagnostic message on line 192 also references them.
User decision #18: Remove USERNAME/PASSWORD from train_factorial.yaml.
IMPORTANT: This is a declarative config change explicitly authorized by the user (Rule #31 satisfied).
task.tdd-approach: RED: Add test asserting train_factorial.yaml does NOT contain MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME or MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD. GREEN: Remove the two env var lines and the diagnostic echo line that references them. Keep MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI (still needed).
task.verification: 1. grep -c "MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME\|MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD" \ deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml → 0 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Pin evidently + beartype versions
task.description:
evidently and beartype have had breaking API changes across minor versions. Unpinned versions cause import errors and test failures on fresh installs. Pin to known-working versions in pyproject.toml extras.
User decision #19: Pin versions.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that imports evidently and beartype, checks their __version__ against the pinned range in pyproject.toml. GREEN: Pin evidently and beartype in pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies] to their current known-working versions. Run uv sync --all-extras.
task.verification: 1. uv sync --all-extras succeeds 2. uv run python -c "import evidently; print(evidently.__version__)" → pinned version 3. uv run python -c "import beartype; print(beartype.__version__)" → pinned version 4. make test-staging passes

phase.name: Code Fixes
phase.description: Fix all silent failures, hardcoded parameters, metric key mismatches, unwired functions, and duplicate code patterns. Each fix follows TDD and addresses a specific CLAUDE.md rule violation.

task.title: Central MetricKeys constants module — verify completeness
task.description:
MetricKeys in src/minivess/observability/metric_keys.py exists (#790) but not all flow files use it consistently. Some flows still hardcode metric key strings like "eval/fold0/dice" or "val/loss" instead of importing from MetricKeys. Audit all flows and replace hardcoded metric key strings with MetricKeys constants.
User decision #6: Central MetricKeys constants module.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write AST-based test that scans all flow files for hardcoded metric key strings (patterns like "train/loss", "val/dice", "eval/fold" etc.) that are NOT imported from MetricKeys. Test fails because hardcoded strings exist. GREEN: Replace every hardcoded metric key string with the corresponding MetricKeys constant. Add missing keys to MetricKeys if needed.
task.verification: 1. AST-based guard test passes (no hardcoded metric strings in flows) 2. make test-staging passes 3. grep -rn '"train/\|"val/\|"eval/' src/minivess/orchestration/flows/ → only imports

task.title: Fix metric key mismatch in builder.py
task.description: builder.py logs metrics with keys that don't match MetricKeys constants. This causes downstream consumers (analysis_flow, biostatistics_flow) to query for keys that were never logged, resulting in empty results that are silently discarded. The mismatch is between the key used in mlflow.log_metric() and the key used in MlflowClient.search_runs() filter_string.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that instantiates builder and verifies all logged metric keys match MetricKeys constants exactly. GREEN: Replace all hardcoded metric key strings in builder.py with MetricKeys imports.
task.verification: 1. New test passes 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: SWAG plugin — raise PostTrainingPluginError instead of returning empty
task.description:
When SWAG fails (e.g., insufficient epochs, NaN divergence), the plugin silently returns an empty dict {} instead of raising an exception. This violates CLAUDE.md Rule #25 (Loud Failures, Never Silent Discards). The post_training_flow receives empty results and logs nothing, making the failure invisible in MLflow.
User decision #4: Raise exceptions for silent failures. User decision #16: SWAG stays in debug (Rule 27).
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that triggers SWAG failure conditions (empty model, zero posterior samples) and asserts PostTrainingPluginError is raised. Test fails because plugin returns {} silently. GREEN: Replace all `return {}` paths in swag.py with `raise PostTrainingPluginError("SWAG failed: {reason}")`. Add PostTrainingPluginError to the plugin base module if not present.
task.verification: 1. Test verifies exceptions are raised for every failure mode 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: DataLoader fallback — raise instead of falling back to raw pairs
task.description: When DataLoader creation fails (e.g., transform error, shape mismatch), the training loop silently falls back to raw image/label pairs without transforms. This produces misleading training metrics (no augmentation, no normalization) and violates Rule #25. The fallback was a debugging convenience that should never have been committed.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that mocks DataLoader creation to fail and asserts that the training flow raises an exception (not falls back silently). GREEN: Remove the raw-pairs fallback path. Replace with raise ValueError("DataLoader creation failed: {original_error}").
task.verification: 1. Test verifies exception propagation 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Hardcoded tracking_uri "mlruns" fallback in train_flow.py → resolve_tracking_uri()
task.description:
train_flow.py line ~186 uses os.environ.get("DVC_REMOTE", "minio") and similar fallback patterns throughout. The tracking URI resolution must use resolve_tracking_uri() which fails loudly if MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI is not set, as documented in observability/CLAUDE.md.
User decision #9: Fail loudly, no silent defaults.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test scanning train_flow.py for os.environ.get() calls with tracking URI fallbacks. Test fails because fallbacks exist. GREEN: Replace os.environ.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI", "mlruns") with resolve_tracking_uri(). Ensure resolve_tracking_uri() raises if env var is unset (it should already, verify).
task.verification: 1. grep "mlruns" src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py → 0 hits (except comments) 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Central CHECKPOINT_BEST_FILENAME constant — verify all usages
task.description:
CHECKPOINT_BEST_FILENAME = "best_val_loss.pth" exists in orchestration/constants.py but some files may still hardcode the string "best_val_loss.pth" or use variant names like "best_model.pth". All checkpoint filename references must use the constant.
User decision #14: Central constants in orchestration/constants.py.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write AST-based test scanning all flow files for hardcoded "best_val_loss.pth" or "best_model.pth" strings not imported from constants. Test fails if hardcoded strings found. GREEN: Replace all hardcoded checkpoint filenames with CHECKPOINT_BEST_FILENAME constant from orchestration/constants.py.
task.verification: 1. AST guard test passes 2. grep -rn "best_val_loss.pth\|best_model.pth" src/minivess/orchestration/flows/ → only imports 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Fix checkpoint naming in post_training_flow.py
task.description: post_training_flow.py may save post-training checkpoints (SWAG, SWA) with a different naming convention than what analysis_flow.py expects when discovering checkpoints. Both must use constants from orchestration/constants.py.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that verifies post_training_flow checkpoint save path uses constants from orchestration/constants.py. GREEN: Add post-training checkpoint constants to constants.py (e.g., CHECKPOINT_SWAG_FILENAME = "swag_posterior.pth") and use them in post_training_flow.py.
task.verification: 1. Test passes 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Calibration metrics crash — MetaTensor .cpu().detach().numpy()
task.description:
Calibration metrics (ECE, Brier, etc.) call .numpy() on MONAI MetaTensor objects that may still be on GPU or require gradient detach. This causes: - RuntimeError: Can't call numpy() on Tensor that requires grad - RuntimeError: Expected a torch.Tensor on CPU
Fix: Always chain .cpu().detach().numpy() before passing tensors to numpy-based calibration functions.
User decision #12: Fix .numpy() + verification tests.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that creates GPU tensors with requires_grad=True, passes them to calibration metrics, and verifies no RuntimeError. Test fails because .numpy() is called without .cpu().detach(). GREEN: Add defensive .cpu().detach().numpy() chain to all calibration metric functions that convert tensors to numpy arrays. Use a helper function _to_numpy(tensor) to centralize the pattern.
task.verification: 1. Test passes on GPU (if available) and CPU 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Calibration metrics verification tests
task.description: Add comprehensive tests for all calibration metrics (ECE, MCE, Brier, NLL, BUC, pECE, BA-ECE) with known-answer inputs. Verify that: 1. Perfect predictions → ECE ≈ 0 2. Random predictions → ECE ≈ 0.25-0.5 3. Deliberately miscalibrated → ECE > 0.1 4. Results are numerically stable (no NaN/Inf)
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write known-answer tests. Tests pass if calibration module is correct. GREEN: Fix any calibration metric bugs exposed by known-answer tests.
task.verification: 1. All known-answer tests pass 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Remove all 50+ hardcoded alpha/seed/n_bootstrap defaults
task.description:
CLAUDE.md Rule #29: ZERO Hardcoded Parameters. Across 20+ files, there are 38+ occurrences of hardcoded alpha=0.05, seed=42, n_bootstrap=1000, n_permutations=9999 in function signatures. These must ALL be removed and replaced with config-driven parameters.
Files with hardcoded defaults (from grep): - biostatistics_statistics.py (5 occurrences) - biostatistics_rankings.py (2) - biostatistics_figures.py (2) - biostatistics_specification_curve.py (3) - comparison.py (4) - comparison_report.py (2) - topology_comparison.py (2) - evaluation.py (1) - calibration_metrics.py (1) - data/splits.py (2 — seed=42) - data/drift_simulation_setup.py (3) - data/acquisition_simulator.py (1) - data/debug_dataset.py (1 — seed=42) - testing/debug_data_config.py (1 — seed=42) - post_training_plugins/subsampled_ensemble.py (1) - agents/acquisition/conformal_bandit.py (1) - orchestration/flows/biostatistics_flow.py (3) - orchestration/flows/data_flow.py (1) - orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py (1) - observability/pprm.py (1)
User decision #5: Fix ALL 50+ instances.
task.tdd-approach: RED: tests/v2/unit/biostatistics/test_no_hardcoded_alpha.py already exists (or create it). AST-based scan of ALL src/minivess/ files for function signatures containing default values for alpha, seed, n_bootstrap, n_permutations. Test fails for all 50+ occurrences. GREEN: For each function: 1. Remove the default value from the signature 2. Make the parameter required (caller must pass it) 3. Update all callers to pass the value from their config object 4. Ensure config dataclasses (BiostatisticsConfig, TrainingConfig, etc.) define the canonical defaults
task.verification: 1. AST guard test passes (zero hardcoded alpha/seed/n_bootstrap in signatures) 2. All existing tests still pass (they must read defaults from config) 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: 7 conformal alpha=0.1 hardcodes → parameterize
task.description: Conformal prediction code uses alpha=0.1 (90% coverage) as a hardcoded default in function signatures. This is a researcher-configurable parameter that must come from config. Separate from #881 because conformal alpha is a different parameter from statistical alpha=0.05.
task.tdd-approach: RED: AST-based test scanning conformal modules for hardcoded alpha defaults. Test fails for all 7 occurrences. GREEN: Add conformal_alpha to relevant config class. Remove defaults from function signatures. Update callers.
task.verification: 1. Guard test passes 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: 13 os.environ.get() fallbacks in train_flow.py → fail loudly
task.description:
train_flow.py has ~20 os.environ.get() calls, 13 of which provide silent fallback defaults (e.g., os.environ.get("DVC_REMOTE", "minio"), os.environ.get("SPLITS_DIR", "configs/splits")). Per CLAUDE.md Rule #22 and observability/CLAUDE.md, flow files must NOT have silent fallbacks. Either use a resolve_*() function or fail loudly with a clear message.
User decision #9: Fail loudly, no silent defaults.
Specific offenders (from grep of train_flow.py): - Line 184: os.environ.get("DATA_DIR", "/app/data") - Line 186: os.environ.get("DVC_REMOTE", "minio") - Line 1152: os.environ.get("SPLITS_DIR", "configs/splits") - Line 1303: os.environ.get("LOGS_DIR", "/app/logs") - Line 1330: os.environ.get("SPLITS_DIR", "configs/splits") - Line 1331: os.environ.get("CHECKPOINT_DIR", "/app/checkpoints") - Line 1457-1539: Multiple argparse defaults with os.environ.get()
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write AST-based test scanning all flow files for os.environ.get() calls with two arguments (indicating a fallback default). Test fails for all 13 occurrences. GREEN: Create env_resolution.py with typed resolve_*() functions: - resolve_data_dir() → Path (raises if DATA_DIR unset) - resolve_splits_dir() → Path - resolve_checkpoint_dir() → Path - resolve_logs_dir() → Path - resolve_dvc_remote() → str Replace all os.environ.get() with fallbacks in train_flow.py with these resolve functions. For argparse defaults, use env var without fallback: os.environ["VAR"] (crashes if missing, which is correct).
task.verification: 1. AST guard test passes (zero os.environ.get with 2 args in flow files) 2. Tests with proper env vars set still pass 3. Tests without env vars → clear error messages 4. make test-staging passes

task.title: Wire 7 unwired production functions to flow entry points
task.description:
7 production-grade functions exist in the codebase but are not called from any Prefect flow entry point. They are dead code that was written but never wired into the execution path. Each must be wired into the appropriate flow.
User decision #10: Wire all 7.
The specific unwired functions need to be identified via a code audit: likely candidates include embedding_drift, comparison_report, topology_comparison, specification_curve, and similar analysis/ biostatistics functions.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that traces the call graph from each flow's @flow function and asserts all production functions in src/minivess/pipeline/ are reachable. Test fails for the 7 unwired functions. GREEN: Add @task wrappers and calls to each unwired function in the appropriate flow. Use try/except with logger.error() for optional analyses (e.g., embedding_drift), and direct calls for required analyses (e.g., comparison_report).
task.verification: 1. All 7 functions are called from at least one flow 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Hardcoded experiment names in deploy_flow and dashboard_flow
task.description: deploy_flow.py and dashboard_flow.py may hardcode MLflow experiment name strings instead of importing from orchestration/constants.py (EXPERIMENT_DEPLOYMENT, EXPERIMENT_DASHBOARD). This causes silent cross-flow isolation failures when the experiment name convention changes.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write AST-based test scanning flow files for hardcoded strings matching "minivess_*" that should be EXPERIMENT_* constants. GREEN: Replace all hardcoded experiment name strings with constants.
task.verification: 1. Guard test passes 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Nemenyi q_alpha table → parameterize with scipy.stats
task.description:
biostatistics_rankings.py contains a hardcoded q_alpha critical value lookup table for the Nemenyi post-hoc test. This should use scipy.stats.studentized_range for dynamic computation based on the number of groups and significance level, rather than a static table that only works for specific k values.
Rule #3 (Library-First): scipy.stats has this.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test computing Nemenyi critical difference for known k and alpha values, comparing against published table values. GREEN: Replace hardcoded q_alpha table with scipy.stats.studentized_range computation. Parameterize alpha from config.
task.verification: 1. Known-answer tests match published critical difference tables 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Bare except:pass in train_flow.py → proper exception handling
task.description:
train_flow.py has multiple bare except: blocks (lines ~284, 689, 881, 910, 932, 1050) that silently swallow exceptions. These hide real errors (OOM, CUDA errors, data corruption) and make debugging impossible. Each must be replaced with specific exception types and proper logging.
Rule #25: Loud Failures, Never Silent Discards.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write AST-based test scanning train_flow.py for bare except: or except Exception: blocks that contain only pass/continue/return. Test fails for all 6 offending blocks. GREEN: For each bare except: 1. Identify the specific exception types that can occur 2. Replace with specific except (RuntimeError, ValueError, etc.) 3. Add logger.error() with context information 4. Re-raise if the error is fatal (OOM, CUDA) 5. Log and continue only for truly optional operations (e.g., GPU temp query)
task.verification: 1. AST guard test passes (zero bare except: blocks) 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Duplicate code: flow epilogue pattern (175 lines x 5 flows)
task.description: All 5 Prefect flows have near-identical epilogue code (~175 lines each): MLflow run finalization, lineage emission, audit trail logging, error handling, and status reporting. This is ~875 lines of duplicated code that should be extracted into a shared utility.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test for a flow_epilogue() utility function that handles MLflow finalization, lineage emission, audit logging, and error handling. Test fails because the utility doesn't exist. GREEN: Create flow_epilogue.py with a shared FlowEpilogue context manager or decorator. Refactor all 5 flows to use it.
task.verification: 1. flow_epilogue utility tests pass 2. All 5 flows use the shared utility 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Duplicate code: MLflow run setup boilerplate (120 lines x 8)
task.description: MLflow run initialization (set experiment, start run, log params, set tags, configure autolog) is repeated ~120 lines x 8 times across flow files. Extract into a shared mlflow_run_context() utility.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test for an mlflow_run_context() context manager that handles experiment setup, run creation, and param logging. GREEN: Implement mlflow_run_context() in mlflow_helpers.py. Refactor flows to use it (start with one flow, verify, then batch).
task.verification: 1. mlflow_run_context utility tests pass 2. At least 3 flows refactored to use it 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: n_matched ambiguity in component_dice topology metrics
task.description: topology_comparison.py has an n_matched variable used in component_dice computation where it's ambiguous whether it counts matched components from prediction→ground-truth or ground-truth→prediction. This can silently produce incorrect topology metrics. Add explicit directional naming and documentation.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test with asymmetric topology (pred has 5 components, GT has 3) verifying that pred→GT and GT→pred matching counts differ. GREEN: Rename n_matched to n_matched_pred_to_gt and n_matched_gt_to_pred. Add docstring clarifying the direction.
task.verification: 1. Directional test passes with correct counts 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: Augmentation hyperparameters → config-driven
task.description:
Data augmentation transforms (random flip probability, rotation range, intensity shift range, etc.) have hardcoded values in the transform pipeline construction code. These should be config-driven via the Hydra config chain so researchers can tune augmentation without code changes.
Rule #29: ZERO Hardcoded Parameters.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that verifies augmentation parameters are read from config, not hardcoded in transforms.py. GREEN: Add AugmentationConfig dataclass to config/models.py. Move all augmentation hyperparameters to configs/augmentation/default.yaml. Update transforms.py to accept AugmentationConfig.
task.verification: 1. Config-driven test passes 2. Changing augmentation YAML changes the transforms 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: VRAM overhead constants → profile YAML
task.description: VRAM overhead estimates for model selection (e.g., "SAM3 needs 16GB", "DynUNet needs 4GB") are hardcoded as constants in model adapter code or VRAM check scripts. These should be in a profile YAML so they can be updated as models evolve without code changes.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that reads VRAM requirements from YAML profile and asserts they match the values used in sam3_vram_check.py. GREEN: Create configs/hardware/vram_profiles.yaml with per-model VRAM requirements. Update sam3_vram_check.py to read from YAML.
task.verification: 1. VRAM profile test passes 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: HPO flow lr=1e-3 vs TrainingConfig lr=1e-4 shadow default
task.description: The HPO flow search space may define learning rate range starting at 1e-3, while TrainingConfig has a default of 1e-4. When HPO suggests a value, it's unclear whether the HPO suggestion or the config default takes precedence. This is a shadow default bug: the researcher thinks they're searching over lr ∈ [1e-5, 1e-2] but the config default silently overrides the HPO suggestion if the override chain is broken.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that creates an HPO trial with lr=1e-3 and asserts the actual training config used for that trial has lr=1e-3 (not the config default of 1e-4). GREEN: Ensure HPO override chain explicitly sets TrainingConfig.lr from the Optuna suggestion. Remove any default that could shadow it.
task.verification: 1. Shadow default test passes 2. make test-staging passes

task.title: DVC_REMOTE="minio" default in train_flow → fail loudly
task.description:
train_flow.py line ~186: os.environ.get("DVC_REMOTE", "minio") silently defaults to MinIO when DVC_REMOTE is unset. On GCP, this causes training to attempt pulling data from a non-existent MinIO instance instead of GCS, resulting in a silent data fetch failure followed by training on empty data.
This is a specific instance of Task 2.12 but called out separately because it caused actual data loss on the 5th factorial pass.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that unsets DVC_REMOTE env var and calls the DVC remote resolution function. Test asserts ValueError is raised. GREEN: Replace os.environ.get("DVC_REMOTE", "minio") with a resolve_dvc_remote() function that raises if DVC_REMOTE is unset.
task.verification: 1. Test passes (ValueError raised when DVC_REMOTE unset) 2. grep "minio" train_flow.py → only in comments, not as default 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Orphan YAML config keys (defined but never read)
task.description: Audit all Hydra config YAML files for keys that are defined but never read by any Python code. These orphan keys are confusing for researchers (they think changing the key does something, but it doesn't) and indicate wiring gaps. Remove orphan keys or wire them properly.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write test that: 1. Collects all keys from Hydra YAML config files 2. Scans all Python source for references to those keys 3. Reports keys defined in YAML but never referenced in Python Test fails if orphan keys exist. GREEN: For each orphan key, either: (a) Wire it to the appropriate Python code, or (b) Remove it from the YAML config with a comment explaining why
task.verification: 1. Orphan key test passes (or has explicit allowed_orphans list) 2. make test-staging passes

phase.name: Validation

task.title: Local mock E2E test (train → analysis → biostatistics)
task.description:
Create a local mock E2E integration test that exercises the full pipeline chain: train_flow → post_training_flow → analysis_flow → biostatistics_flow → dashboard_flow. Uses mock data (tiny volumes), mock MLflow (file-based), and skips actual GPU training (mock model outputs). Verifies that: 1. Each flow writes expected MLflow artifacts 2. Downstream flows can discover upstream runs 3. Metric keys match between producer and consumer 4. No silent failures in the chain
User decision #13: Local mock test + cloud E2E plan.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write the E2E test with mock data. Test exercises each flow sequentially and asserts artifacts + metrics at each stage. GREEN: Fix any wiring issues exposed by the E2E test (likely several, given that many functions are unwired per Task 2.13).
task.verification: 1. E2E mock test passes locally (no GPU needed) 2. make test-staging passes (test is included in staging tier)

task.title: Cloud E2E plan for after training completes
task.description:
Document the cloud E2E verification plan as a checklist: 1. sky jobs launch train_factorial.yaml (1 condition, 2 epochs) 2. Verify MLflow Cloud Run has the run with correct metrics 3. Verify GCS artifacts bucket has artifacts 4. Verify GCS checkpoints bucket has checkpoint 5. Run analysis_flow on the completed run 6. Run biostatistics_flow on analysis results 7. Run dashboard_flow to generate report
This is a PLAN (not automated test) because it requires GCP infra.
task.tdd-approach: N/A — this is a documentation task. The checklist IS the verification.
task.verification: 1. Checklist document exists and covers all 7 steps 2. Each step has expected outcomes and failure troubleshooting

task.title: MambaVesselNet cloud smoke test
task.description:
Add MambaVesselNet to the cloud smoke test suite. Verify that the model loads, runs forward pass on 1 volume, and produces valid segmentation output on a GCP L4 GPU. This catches import errors, CUDA compatibility issues, and VRAM OOM before committing to a full factorial run.
User decision #7: Include cloud smoke test for MambaVesselNet.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write smoke test that imports MambaVesselNet adapter, creates a tiny input tensor, runs forward pass, asserts output shape. GREEN: Ensure MambaVesselNet adapter handles L4 GPU correctly (BF16 support, VRAM bounds). Fix any import or compatibility issues.
task.verification: 1. sky jobs launch smoke_test_mamba.yaml succeeds on GCP L4 2. Test log shows valid forward pass output

task.title: Guard test: all flows use MetricKeys constants
task.description:
AST-based guard test that scans ALL flow files (src/minivess/orchestration/flows/*.py) and pipeline files (src/minivess/pipeline/*.py) for hardcoded metric key strings. Any string matching the pattern "train/", "val/", "eval/", "optim/", "grad/", "gpu/", "cost/", "sys/" that is not imported from MetricKeys is a violation.
This is the permanent guard that prevents regression of Task 2.1.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write the guard test. It should fail if ANY hardcoded metric key string exists in flow or pipeline files. GREEN: Already handled by Task 2.1 (this test ensures it stays fixed).
task.verification: 1. Guard test passes 2. Adding a hardcoded metric key to any flow makes the test fail 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Guard test: no hardcoded alpha/seed in function signatures
task.description:
AST-based guard test that scans ALL src/minivess/ files for function definitions with default parameter values for alpha, seed, n_bootstrap, n_permutations, conformal_alpha, or any statistical/random parameter.
This is the permanent guard for #881 (Rule #29).
task.tdd-approach: RED: Test should already exist per #881. If not, create it. GREEN: Already handled by Task 2.10.
task.verification: 1. Guard test passes 2. Adding alpha=0.05 to any function makes the test fail 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Guard test: no os.environ.get() with fallbacks in flow files
task.description:
AST-based guard test scanning all files in src/minivess/orchestration/flows/ for os.environ.get() calls with two arguments (indicating a fallback default). Only os.environ.get() with one argument (returns None) or os.environ["VAR"] (raises KeyError) are allowed.
This is the permanent guard for Task 2.12.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write AST-based guard test. Fails if any flow file has os.environ.get("VAR", "fallback") pattern. GREEN: Already handled by Task 2.12.
task.verification: 1. Guard test passes 2. Adding os.environ.get("X", "y") to any flow makes test fail 3. make test-staging passes

task.title: Verification script: check all GitHub issues addressed
task.description:
Create a verification script (scripts/verify_8th_pass_backlog.py) that: 1. Reads this XML plan 2. For each task, runs the associated verification checks 3. Reports PASS/FAIL for each task 4. Cross-references GitHub issues to ensure all are created 5. Produces a summary table
User decision #17: GitHub issues + XML checklist with verification script.
task.tdd-approach: N/A — this is a utility script. It IS the verification tool.
task.verification: 1. Script runs without errors 2. Produces clear PASS/FAIL report for each task 3. Exits with non-zero code if any task is FAIL

task.title: make test-prod with zero-skip enforcement
task.description:
Run make test-prod and enforce zero skips (Rule #28). Every skipped test must be investigated: - Module not installed → uv sync --all-extras - Hardware-specific (GPU) → acceptable with documentation - Deprecated library → delete the test - Cloud credentials → acceptable for local dev
Report the full skip inventory with reasons.
task.tdd-approach: N/A — this is an execution + investigation task. For each skip, follow the Skip Investigation Protocol (Rule #28).
task.verification: 1. make test-prod passes with documented skip count 2. Each skip has a documented reason (hardware, cloud, or fixed) 3. Zero non-hardware, non-cloud skips

task.title: Flaky Prefect SQLite tests (#880) investigation
task.description:
Issue #880 reports intermittent SQLite locking errors in Prefect tests. Root cause investigation needed: 1. Is it WAL mode vs DELETE mode? 2. Is it parallel test execution (pytest-xdist)? 3. Is it tmp directory cleanup race? 4. Is it Prefect's own SQLite connection pool?
This is an investigation task, not necessarily a fix. The outcome should be a root cause analysis and fix recommendation.
task.tdd-approach: RED: Write a stress test that reproduces the flaky behavior by running multiple Prefect operations concurrently. GREEN: Fix based on root cause (likely: set WAL mode, or use tmpdir fixture instead of shared tmp, or disable xdist for Prefect tests).
task.verification: 1. Stress test passes 100/100 runs 2. make test-staging shows no intermittent Prefect SQLite failures

github-issues.description:
All 38 items that need GitHub issues. User decision #20: Create GitHub issues for all untracked findings. Existing issues (#878, #880, #881) already exist; the remaining 35 are new.
Labels: P0-critical, P1-important, infra, code-quality, silent-failure, rule-violation, testing, config, metrics, documentation

issue.title: #878: MLflow GCS artifact store via Pulumi
issue.body: MLflow on Cloud Run missing --default-artifact-root gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts. Fix: Pulumi __main__.py Cloud Run service update. Plan: run-debug-factorial-experiment-report-8th-pass-backlog-fix-plan.xml Task 1.1

issue.title: #880: Flaky Prefect SQLite tests
issue.body: Intermittent SQLite locking errors in Prefect tests. Plan: Task 3.9 — root cause investigation.

issue.title: #881: Remove all hardcoded alpha/seed/n_bootstrap defaults
issue.body: 50+ hardcoded defaults across 20 files. Rule #29 violation. Plan: Task 2.10 (fix) + Task 3.5 (guard test).

issue.title: GCP GPU quota increase to 4 for parallel factorial runs
issue.body: Current GPUS_ALL_REGIONS quota is 1. Request increase to 4 for parallel factorial condition execution (~4x wall-clock reduction). Update gcp_quotas.yaml and preflight_gcp.py. Plan: Task 1.2

issue.title: train_production.yaml stale — still uses GHCR + RunPod
issue.body: train_production.yaml references GHCR image and RunPod cloud instead of GAR and GCP. Must be updated to mirror train_factorial.yaml infrastructure. Plan: Task 1.3

issue.title: train_hpo.yaml stale — GHCR + UpCloud references
issue.body: train_hpo.yaml references GHCR image, RunPod cloud, and UpCloud S3 credentials. UpCloud was sunset 2026-03-16. Must use GAR + GCP + GCS. Plan: Task 1.4

issue.title: Remove vestigial MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME/PASSWORD from train_factorial.yaml
issue.body: MLflow 3.x uses IAM auth, not HTTP basic auth. USERNAME/PASSWORD env vars in train_factorial.yaml lines 77-78 are vestigial and confusing. Plan: Task 1.5

issue.title: Pin evidently + beartype versions in pyproject.toml
issue.body: Unpinned evidently and beartype cause import errors on fresh installs. Pin to known-working versions. Plan: Task 1.6

issue.title: builder.py metric key mismatch with MetricKeys constants
issue.body: builder.py logs metrics with keys that don't match MetricKeys constants. Downstream consumers query wrong keys → empty results silently discarded. Plan: Task 2.2

issue.title: SWAG plugin silently returns empty dict on failure
issue.body: SWAG plugin returns {} on failure instead of raising PostTrainingPluginError. Violates Rule #25 (Loud Failures). Post-training flow logs nothing, failure invisible. Plan: Task 2.3

issue.title: DataLoader silent fallback to raw pairs without transforms
issue.body: When DataLoader creation fails, training silently falls back to raw image/label pairs without augmentation/normalization. Produces misleading metrics. Rule #25. Plan: Task 2.4

issue.title: Hardcoded "mlruns" tracking URI fallback in flow files
issue.body: Flow files use os.environ.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI", "mlruns") instead of resolve_tracking_uri(). On GCP, this silently logs to ephemeral local mlruns/. Rules #22, #25. observability/CLAUDE.md says "use resolve_tracking_uri()". Plan: Task 2.5

issue.title: Hardcoded checkpoint filenames in flow files
issue.body: Some flow files hardcode "best_val_loss.pth" instead of importing CHECKPOINT_BEST_FILENAME from orchestration/constants.py. Plan: Task 2.6

issue.title: Post-training checkpoint naming not using constants
issue.body: post_training_flow.py saves SWAG checkpoints with ad-hoc names. Must use constants from orchestration/constants.py for cross-flow contract. Plan: Task 2.7

issue.title: Calibration metrics crash on GPU MetaTensor .numpy()
issue.body: Calibration metrics call .numpy() on MONAI MetaTensor that may be on GPU or require gradient detach. Causes RuntimeError. Fix: .cpu().detach().numpy(). Plan: Task 2.8

issue.title: Calibration metrics missing known-answer verification tests
issue.body: ECE, MCE, Brier, NLL, BUC, pECE, BA-ECE have no known-answer tests. Need tests with perfect/random/miscalibrated inputs to verify correctness. Plan: Task 2.9

issue.title: 7 conformal alpha=0.1 hardcodes need parameterization
issue.body: Conformal prediction code hardcodes alpha=0.1 (90% coverage) in function signatures. Must be config-driven per Rule #29. Plan: Task 2.11

issue.title: 13 os.environ.get() with silent fallbacks in train_flow.py
issue.body: train_flow.py has 13 os.environ.get() calls with fallback defaults that mask misconfiguration. Must fail loudly per Rules #22, #25. Plan: Task 2.12

issue.title: 7 unwired production functions not called from any flow
issue.body: 7 production-grade functions exist but are never called from Prefect flow entry points. Dead code that should be wired into the appropriate flows. Plan: Task 2.13

issue.title: Hardcoded experiment names in deploy_flow and dashboard_flow
issue.body: deploy_flow.py and dashboard_flow.py hardcode MLflow experiment name strings instead of importing EXPERIMENT_* constants from orchestration/constants.py. Plan: Task 2.14

issue.title: Nemenyi q_alpha table hardcoded — use scipy.stats
issue.body: biostatistics_rankings.py has a hardcoded q_alpha lookup table for Nemenyi test. Replace with scipy.stats.studentized_range per Rule #3 (Library-First). Plan: Task 2.15

issue.title: 6 bare except:pass blocks in train_flow.py swallow exceptions
issue.body: train_flow.py has 6 bare except: blocks (lines ~284, 689, 881, 910, 932, 1050) that silently swallow exceptions including OOM and CUDA errors. Rule #25. Plan: Task 2.16

issue.title: Flow epilogue pattern duplicated 175 lines x 5 flows
issue.body: All 5 Prefect flows have near-identical ~175-line epilogue code for MLflow finalization, lineage, audit, error handling. Extract shared utility. Plan: Task 2.17

issue.title: MLflow run setup boilerplate duplicated 120 lines x 8
issue.body: MLflow run initialization is repeated ~120 lines across 8 entry points. Extract mlflow_run_context() context manager into mlflow_helpers.py. Plan: Task 2.18

issue.title: n_matched ambiguity in component_dice topology metrics
issue.body: topology_comparison.py n_matched is ambiguous — pred→GT or GT→pred direction? Rename to directional names and add docstring. Plan: Task 2.19

issue.title: Augmentation hyperparameters hardcoded — make config-driven
issue.body: Data augmentation transform parameters (flip prob, rotation range, etc.) are hardcoded in transforms.py. Must be config-driven per Rule #29. Plan: Task 2.20

issue.title: VRAM overhead constants hardcoded — move to profile YAML
issue.body: Per-model VRAM requirements hardcoded in sam3_vram_check.py. Move to configs/hardware/vram_profiles.yaml. Plan: Task 2.21

issue.title: HPO flow lr=1e-3 shadow default over TrainingConfig lr=1e-4
issue.body: HPO flow search space and TrainingConfig have different lr defaults. Unclear which takes precedence, potentially silently overriding HPO suggestions. Plan: Task 2.22

issue.title: DVC_REMOTE="minio" silent default in train_flow.py
issue.body: os.environ.get("DVC_REMOTE", "minio") silently defaults to MinIO on GCP, causing data fetch failure. Caused actual data loss on 5th factorial pass. Plan: Task 2.23

issue.title: Orphan YAML config keys — defined but never read
issue.body: Audit needed: some Hydra config YAML keys are defined but never read by Python code. Confuses researchers. Remove or wire them. Plan: Task 2.24

issue.title: Local mock E2E test for full pipeline chain
issue.body: Need local mock E2E integration test: train → post_training → analysis → biostatistics → dashboard. Verifies inter-flow contracts, metric key consistency, and no silent failures without requiring GPU. Plan: Task 3.1

issue.title: Cloud E2E verification checklist document
issue.body: Document the cloud E2E verification steps for post-training validation on GCP. 7-step checklist from SkyPilot launch to dashboard report. Plan: Task 3.2

issue.title: MambaVesselNet cloud smoke test on GCP L4
issue.body: Add MambaVesselNet to cloud smoke test suite. Verify model loads and runs forward pass on GCP L4 before committing to full factorial. Plan: Task 3.3

issue.title: Guard test: all flows use MetricKeys constants (no hardcoded strings)
issue.body: AST-based guard test preventing regression of hardcoded metric key strings in flow and pipeline files. Permanent guard for MetricKeys adoption. Plan: Task 3.4

issue.title: Guard test: no os.environ.get() with fallbacks in flows
issue.body: AST-based guard test preventing regression of silent env var fallbacks in flow files. Permanent guard for Rule #22. Plan: Task 3.6

issue.title: Verification script for 8th pass backlog completion
issue.body: Script that reads the XML plan, runs verification checks for each task, and reports PASS/FAIL summary. Ensures all 37 issues are addressed. Plan: Task 3.7

issue.title: make test-prod zero-skip enforcement audit
issue.body: Run make test-prod and investigate every skip per Rule #28 Skip Investigation Protocol. Zero non-hardware, non-cloud skips accepted. Plan: Task 3.8

checklist.description: Master checklist mapping every issue to its task. Updated as tasks complete. Status values: TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE, BLOCKED.
checklist.item: Remove MLflow auth env vars from train_factorial.yaml
Fix metric key mismatch in builder.py
SWAG plugin — raise instead of returning empty
DataLoader fallback — raise instead of raw pairs

verification-script.description: Specification for scripts/verify_8th_pass_backlog.py — the automated verification tool that checks all tasks are complete.

checks.check: Parse deployment/pulumi/gcp/__main__.py. Assert Cloud Run container args contain "--default-artifact-root" with "gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts".
Parse configs/cloud/gcp_quotas.yaml. Assert expected_gpus_all_regions >= 4.
Parse deployment/skypilot/train_production.yaml. Assert image_id contains "europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev" and cloud is "gcp".
Parse deployment/skypilot/train_hpo.yaml. Assert image_id contains "europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev" and no "UpCloud" or "AWS_ACCESS_KEY" strings.
Check deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml for MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME and MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD. Assert zero matches.
Parse pyproject.toml. Assert evidently and beartype have pinned version ranges.
Scan src/minivess/orchestration/flows/*.py for hardcoded metric key strings matching slash-prefix patterns. Assert zero hardcoded strings.
Scan src/minivess/pipeline/builder.py for hardcoded metric key strings. Assert all metric keys imported from MetricKeys.
Scan src/minivess/pipeline/post_training_plugins/swag.py for "return {}" or "return dict()" in except blocks. Assert zero silent returns.
Scan train_flow.py for raw-pairs fallback pattern. Assert not present.
Check train_flow.py for 'os.environ.get.*mlruns' pattern. Assert zero matches.
Scan flow files for hardcoded "best_val_loss.pth" strings. Assert zero (all must use CHECKPOINT_BEST_FILENAME constant).
Verify post_training_flow.py imports checkpoint constants from constants.py.
Scan calibration_metrics.py for bare .numpy() calls. Assert all use .cpu().detach().numpy() or a _to_numpy() helper.
Run pytest tests/v2/unit/pipeline/test_calibration_metrics_known_answers.py. Assert all pass.
Scan ALL src/minivess/*.py function definitions for default values matching alpha=0.05, seed=42, n_bootstrap=1000, n_permutations=9999. Assert zero.
Scan conformal modules for hardcoded alpha defaults. Assert zero.
Scan src/minivess/orchestration/flows/*.py for os.environ.get() with two arguments. Assert zero.
Trace call graph from each @flow function. Assert all production functions in src/minivess/pipeline/ are reachable.
Scan deploy_flow.py and dashboard_flow.py for hardcoded "minivess_*" strings. Assert all use EXPERIMENT_* constants.
Scan biostatistics_rankings.py for hardcoded q_alpha table. Assert replaced with scipy.stats computation.
Scan train_flow.py for bare "except:" or "except Exception:" with only pass/continue/return body. Assert zero.
Assert src/minivess/orchestration/flow_epilogue.py exists.
Assert mlflow_helpers.py contains mlflow_run_context function.
Scan topology_comparison.py for "n_matched" without directional suffix. Assert zero (all must be n_matched_pred_to_gt or n_matched_gt_to_pred).
Assert configs/augmentation/default.yaml exists.
Assert configs/hardware/vram_profiles.yaml exists.
Run pytest tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_hpo_no_shadow_defaults.py.
Check train_flow.py for 'DVC_REMOTE.*minio' with default. Assert zero.
Run pytest tests/v2/unit/config/test_no_orphan_yaml_keys.py.
Run pytest tests/v2/integration/test_e2e_mock_pipeline.py.
Assert docs/planning/cloud-e2e-verification-checklist.md exists.
Assert deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml exists.
Run pytest tests/v2/unit/observability/test_metric_keys_guard.py.
Run pytest tests/v2/unit/biostatistics/test_no_hardcoded_alpha.py.
Run pytest tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_no_env_fallbacks_guard.py.
Assert scripts/verify_8th_pass_backlog.py exists and runs without error.
Run make test-prod. Assert zero non-hardware, non-cloud skips.
Run pytest tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_prefect_sqlite_flaky.py -x --count=10. Assert 10/10 passes.

verification-script.exit-criteria: All 39 checks pass. Zero P0 tasks remain in TODO status. make test-staging passes with zero failures. make test-prod passes with documented skip inventory. All 38 GitHub issues created or updated.

execution-order.description: Optimal execution order respecting task dependencies. Tasks at the same level can be executed in parallel.
